# Léarslán — Data Exploration Report

**Generated by:** `ml/prepare_data.py`  
**Data window:** Jan 2024 – Dec 2024 (latest month snapshot used for ML)

| File | Shape | Granularity | Temporal? |

## 2. Merged DataFrames (saved to `ml/data/`)

| File | Shape | Columns |
| --- | --- | --- |
| county_merged.csv | (26, 17) | county, month, employment_rate, avg_income, unemployment_rate, month_rtb, avg_monthly_rent, rent_growth_pct, rental_yield, month_tii, traffic_volume, congestion_delay_minutes, avg_speed_kph, ber_avg_score, pct_a_rated, pct_bcd_rated, est_annual_energy_cost |
| ed_merged.csv | (255, 20) | ed_id, ed_name, county, month, employment_rate, avg_income, unemployment_rate, month_rtb, avg_monthly_rent, rent_growth_pct, rental_yield, month_tii, traffic_volume, congestion_delay_minutes, avg_speed_kph, ber_avg_score, pct_a_rated, pct_bcd_rated, est_annual_energy_cost, ed_type |

Both files use the **latest available month (Dec 2024)** as the snapshot for ML training. The full 12-month time series is preserved in the raw files for ARIMA forecasting.


## 3. Null / Missing Values

✅ **Zero nulls** across all 8 source files and both merged outputs. No imputation required.

## 6. Zero-Variance Columns (Data Quality Issues)

The following columns have **zero variance** — they are constant across all rows and will contribute nothing to ML models:

| Column | Constant Value | Affected Files | Recommendation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `rent_growth_pct` | 0.08 (8%) everywhere | rtb_rent, rtb_rent_ed | **Drop from feature matrix.** Cannot differentiate areas. Note: the Risk Score formula weights this at 20pts — substitute with a computed YoY rent delta if richer data becomes available. |
| `pct_a_rated` | 15.0 everywhere | seai_ber, seai_ber_ed | **Drop.** No signal. |
| `pct_bcd_rated` | 60.0 everywhere | seai_ber, seai_ber_ed | **Drop.** No signal. |
| `avg_speed_kph` | 60.0 (county) / 50.0 (ED) | tii_traffic, tii_traffic_ed | **Drop.** Constant per spatial level, no within-level variance. |

## 11. Feature Engineering Suggestions

Based on the data exploration, the following derived features are recommended for the ML pipeline:

| Feature Name | Formula | Rationale | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `affordability_index` | `(avg_income / 12) / avg_monthly_rent` | Core affordability signal: >1 means monthly income exceeds rent | 🔴 High |
| `rent_to_income_pct` | `(avg_monthly_rent * 12) / avg_income * 100` | International '30% rule' — >30% = housing-cost-burdened | 🔴 High |
| `commute_cost_monthly` | `congestion_delay_minutes * 2 * 22` | Proxy for monthly commute cost in minutes (22 working days) | 🔴 High |
| `true_cost_index` | `(0.5*Norm(rent) + 0.25*Norm(energy/12) + 0.25*Norm(commute_cost)) * 100` | Composite real cost of living, 0–100 | 🔴 High |
| `energy_tax` | `avg_monthly_rent + (est_annual_energy_cost / 12)` | True monthly housing bill including energy | 🟡 Medium |
| `commute_to_rent_ratio` | `commute_cost_monthly / avg_monthly_rent` | How much of rent is 'spent' on commuting — high = transport-burdened | 🟡 Medium |
| `income_per_rent_unit` | `avg_income / avg_monthly_rent` | Annual income relative to monthly rent — higher = more comfortable | 🟡 Medium |
| `unemployment_burden` | `1 - employment_rate` | Direct inversion for use in Risk Score formula | 🟡 Medium |
| `ber_efficiency` | `1 - ((ber_avg_score - 1) / 6)` | Normalised BER where 1=best (A-rated), 0=worst (G-rated) | 🟡 Medium |
| `rent_per_income_unit` | `avg_monthly_rent / (avg_income / 12)` | Inverse of affordability index — useful as a cost criterion | 🟢 Low |
| `traffic_per_congestion` | `traffic_volume / congestion_delay_minutes` | Road throughput efficiency — high volume + low delay = good infrastructure | 🟢 Low |


## 12. Target Variable Definitions (GBM Self-Supervised)

All four target scores are derived from MinMax-scaled features using weighted formulas. The GBM then learns non-linear interactions on top of these targets.

| Score | Polarity | Key Drivers | Formula Weight Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Risk Score** | 0=safe, 100=dangerous | rent_growth_pct (20), congestion (15), housing_pressure (15), rent_to_income (15), unemployment (15) | 100 |
| **Livability Score** | 0=poor, 100=excellent | employment_rate (20), avg_income (15), BER (15), low_rent (15), supply_score (15) | 100 |
| **Transport Score** | 0=isolated, 100=excellent | low_congestion (40), traffic_volume (30), employment_rate (15), BER (15) | 100 |
| **Affordability Score** | 0=unaffordable, 100=affordable | affordability_index (35), rent_to_income (30), low_rent (20), low_energy (15) | 100 |

> ⚠️ **Note:** `rent_growth_pct` is constant at 0.08 in the current data. The Risk Score formula assigns it 20pts. Until real YoY rent delta data is available, this weight effectively becomes dead weight — consider redistributing to `rental_yield` or `rent_to_income_pct`.


## 13. Recommended Feature Matrix for GBM Training

After dropping zero-variance columns and adding engineered features, the recommended input matrix `X` is:

| # | Feature | Type | Source | Include? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `avg_monthly_rent` | Raw | rtb_rent | ✅ Yes |
| 2 | `avg_income` | Raw | cso_employment | ✅ Yes |
| 3 | `employment_rate` | Raw | cso_employment | ✅ Yes |
| 4 | `unemployment_rate` | Raw | cso_employment | ✅ Yes (redundant with emp_rate but useful for GBM splits) |
| 5 | `traffic_volume` | Raw | tii_traffic | ✅ Yes |
| 6 | `congestion_delay_minutes` | Raw | tii_traffic | ✅ Yes |
| 7 | `ber_avg_score` | Raw | seai_ber | ✅ Yes |
| 8 | `est_annual_energy_cost` | Raw | seai_ber | ✅ Yes |
| 9 | `rental_yield` | Raw | rtb_rent | ✅ Yes |
| 10 | `affordability_index` | Engineered | Derived | ✅ Yes |
| 11 | `rent_to_income_pct` | Engineered | Derived | ✅ Yes |
| 12 | `commute_cost_monthly` | Engineered | Derived | ✅ Yes |
| 13 | `true_cost_index` | Engineered | Derived | ✅ Yes |
| 14 | `energy_tax` | Engineered | Derived | ✅ Yes |
| 15 | `commute_to_rent_ratio` | Engineered | Derived | 🟡 Optional |
| — | `rent_growth_pct` | Raw | rtb_rent | ❌ Drop (zero variance) |
| — | `pct_a_rated` | Raw | seai_ber | ❌ Drop (zero variance) |
| — | `pct_bcd_rated` | Raw | seai_ber | ❌ Drop (zero variance) |
| — | `avg_speed_kph` | Raw | tii_traffic | ❌ Drop (zero variance) |


## 14. ML Algorithm Implementation Plan

| # | Algorithm | Library | Purpose | Input → Output |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | GradientBoostingRegressor × 4 | sklearn | Score risk, livability, transport, affordability | 15 features → 4 × [0–100] scores |
| 2 | KMeans (k=4 county / k=6–8 ED) | sklearn | Neighbourhood archetype clustering | 6 scaled features → cluster ID + label |
| 3 | UMAP | umap-learn | 2D projection for cluster scatter plot | 6 features → (x, y) |
| 4 | IsolationForest | sklearn | Flag anomalous rent spikes / affordability crises | 4 features → anomaly label (-1/1) |
| 5 | TOPSIS (custom) | numpy | Rank areas by user-weighted multi-criteria | 6 criteria + weight vector → closeness score |
| 6 | ARIMA(1,1,1) | statsmodels | 6-month rent/metric forecast per area | 12-month series → forecast + 80% CI |
| 7 | SHAP TreeExplainer | shap | Explain GBM predictions per area | GBM model + feature vector → per-feature contributions |
| 8 | TF-IDF + Cosine Similarity | sklearn | RAG document retrieval for AI Advisor | Query + policy docs → top-3 relevant chunks |


## 15. Recommended Next Steps

1. **`ml/feature_engineering.py`** — implement all V1 + V2 derived features from Section 11

2. **`ml/risk_model.py`** — implement the 4× GBM self-supervised training loop

3. **`ml/clustering.py`** — KMeans + UMAP with semantic archetype labelling

4. **`ml/anomaly_detector.py`** — Isolation Forest with severity classification

5. **`ml/recommender.py`** — TOPSIS with dynamic weight injection

6. **`ml/forecasting.py`** — ARIMA(1,1,1) with linear fallback

7. **`ml/explainability.py`** — SHAP TreeExplainer wrapper

8. **Address `rent_growth_pct` zero-variance issue** — either source real YoY delta data or redistribute its 20pt weight in the Risk Score formula to `rental_yield` and `rent_to_income_pct`
